programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big 'no Substitutions Please' Episode
programme.desc: With Big Dee Dee's pregnancy weighing heavy on her mind, Dee Dee obsesses over the fact that she's about to be replaced by the new baby, so much so, that she finds herself on Phyllis' psychiatrist couch.

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big Forbidden Fruit Episode
programme.desc: After a long dating dry spell, Phyllis reveals that she has a new man, Earl, who seems almost too perfect, but when Dee Dee discovers that Earl is married, Phyllis confesses that she already knew and must decide whether to do the right thing or her thing.

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big Butting In Episode
programme.desc: Encouraging her mother Phyllis to get back out in the dating scene, Mona gets more than she bargained for when Phyllis decides to join Mona’s social circle and to make matters worse, Mona discovers that her mom is better at meeting men than she is.

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big Foot In My Mouth Episode
programme.desc: When Mona discovers a potential new talent Zora (guest star Tamyra Gray), she gets more than she bargains for when Zora makes a move on her.

programme.title: NEW Half & Half: The Big College Admission Episode
programme.desc: After years of claiming to be nothing more than friends, Spencer and Mona are shocked when Spencer’s cousin Chauncey is hired at Delicious Records and recalls the night in college when he caught Mona and Spencer having drunken sex

programme.title: Girlfriends: Take This Poem and Call Me in the Morning
programme.desc: While Ellis mopes about his career, Lynn lies about her past.

programme.title: Girlfriends: Howdy Partner
programme.desc: Joan and William are in for a big shock when their boss announces the name of the firm’s new partner.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: A Prayer for the Dying
programme.desc: A mother of two is found dead in the most expensive part of Colorado Springs, leaving Lt. Joe Kenda stumped—until he looks into her personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Magic Bullet
programme.desc: A hard-working father of two is shot in the head just steps from a notorious strip club. The case is stuck in neutral until Kenda uncovers a conspiracy organized by an unlikely triggerman.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Rearview Mirror
programme.desc: When a retired military man runs over a pedestrian, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda to determine if it was bad brakes or bad intent.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter
programme.desc: When police find 24-year-old Patrick Ray with a gunshot to the temple, the scene has all the telltale signs of suicide. But Lt. Joe Kenda's instinct says otherwise.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Justified
programme.desc: A young man is found gunned down, leading Lt. Joe Kenda to delve into his troubled past and his connection to the world of rare coins.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Widow’s Dogs, Drool Dog, Dexter Follow-up
programme.desc: A retriever mix, and wire hair fox terriers siblings, have turned into a combative pack. A Sharpei/Dobie mix drools excessively. Rottie mix Dexter travels to Los Angeles for a refresher course.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Cesar's Worst Bite
programme.desc: Holly, a Labrador puppy, displays severe food aggression; a women's shelter will take a needy woman and her pit bull, Tucker, but Cesar must help the dog become less protective first.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Fear Factor
programme.desc: Cesar helps dogs overcome their fears including a whippet mix who is blind and deaf, a labradoodle that fears the ocean, and a cocker spaniel that fears all humans.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Tigger and Dan
programme.desc: Cesar comes in to help twins with their biting Chihuahua-Pomeranian mix. A Labrador retriever quickly takes to training to become a hunting dog but has a growing aggressive streak.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Roxie & Foxy, Charlie, and Bubba & Lucky
programme.desc: Two Dachshunds are overprotective of their owners. A couple wants to keep their biting Lhasa Apso. A couple working from home calls in Cesar for some canine conflict resolution.

programme.title: The Dog Whisperer: Miles and Maxie & Booker
programme.desc: Former Guns N’ Roses drummer, Matt Sorum need help controlling his ten month old French bulldog Miles’ aggression. And then, can Cesar clear the name of a dog who killed its sibling?

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Britain: The Russell Murders
programme.desc: Bludgeoned to death whilst walking down a country lane, the horrific murders of Lin and Megan Russell made worldwide news. Discover the events of that shocking day.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Body In The Suitcase: Lorenzo Simon
programme.desc: On 12th May 2014 a human torso was discovered in a suitcase during routine maintenance work on a Birmingham canal. With no useable DNA and no other body parts, the case initially seemed impossible to crack.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Appointment With Death: Scott Singleton
programme.desc: Robbie Coltrane examines the case of Scott Singleton, who murdered a 17-year-old girl after luring her to a bogus job interview.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: The Hitman And The Hairdresser: Fadi Nasri
programme.desc: A 29-year-old hairdresser was found brutally stabbed on her doorstep in a quiet suburban street of London. A recording of a threatening phone call seemed to suggest the motive, but every lead comes to a dead end.

programme.title: Robbie Coltrane's Critical Evidence: Bodies Of Evidence: Roger Severs
programme.desc: In November 1993, Derek and Eileen Severs disappeared without a word. When police spoke to their son, Roger, he insisted they were on holiday. However, there was no evidence of this apparent trip, and Roger was soon arrested on suspicion of murder.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.86
programme.desc: Botched deal to trade babysitting for rent leaves ex-roommates fighting over dumped clothes and unpaid bills. Woman accuses friend of kidnapping her kids during her jail time, refuses to repay dental loan.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.37
programme.desc: Parents allegedly renege on owed money for son's wedding after not being invited/Woman sues child's father for false domestic violence accusation upon discovering love note to wife in Tunisia.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.34
programme.desc: Mother falsely accused of child abuse by ex-husband, his mother, and roommate/Case dismissed after 17 abuse reports/Man sues great nephew for jeep damage; nephew claims fun spin caused crash.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.96
programme.desc: A woman is suing her husband’s ex-wife and teenage daughter for damaging her car when the teenager allegedly pounded it with her fist/A teenager is accused of horsing around and crashing a relative’s Oldsmobile Allero into a curb.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.95
programme.desc: An intoxicated teenager confesses to urinating in a friend’s yard; she sues her ex-friend for coming to her workplace and telling everyone about it.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.19
programme.desc: Man alleges ex-girlfriend ordered escort attack in bar; she denies and countersues for dirt bike balance, car vandalism, and harassment/Trashed hotel room after 18th birthday sparks damages lawsuit.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.194
programme.desc: Jailed during his birthday after being accused of assault and battery while high on drugs, a young man, who was renting a screened-in porch of a mobile home, sues his landlord.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.51
programme.desc: Young nurse sues ex-boyfriend for damages, Dodge Durango payments, motorcycle damages, and unpaid cell phone charges/Woman sues evicted friend and child for back rent, countersued for belongings left behind.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.131
programme.desc: Man sued by parents after second wife's mysterious death and $40,000 life insurance policy; alleged child abuse involved/Man calls cops to remove ex-girlfriend from apartment, sues her for rent after she leaves, fearing for his life.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.30
programme.desc: Man returns to estranged wife after girlfriend allegedly hires divorce attorney/Man sues cousin for damaging and disposing of property post-eviction from shared home.

programme.title: Judge Judy: Episode 13.177
programme.desc: Woman's front teeth cracked when intoxicated dancer trips and knocks into her at bar. Dancer denies wrongdoing. 15-year-old accused of breaking pizzeria window denies involvement but refuses to implicate friend. Police provide different account.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Strange Life of Dr. Schwartz
programme.desc: The investigation into the murder of a beloved Florida doctor reveals his dark, secret past.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Who Killed Jane?
programme.desc: Jane Neumann was found dead of a gunshot wound in the basement of her home in Hudson, Wisconsin. Jane's husband Jim originally told police Jane was murdered by an intruder and then later changed his story and said she committed suicide.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Exposed
programme.desc: A report about serial killer Bill Bradford, who posed as an amateur photographer and told women he’d make them famous. Convicted of two murders, Bradford has been on death row since 1988.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Over the Edge
programme.desc: A report on the investigation into the 2009 disappearance of Dawn Viens, and why her husband, California chef David Viens, jumped off a cliff when he became a suspect in her disappearance.

programme.title: 48 Hours: The Colonel's Wife
programme.desc: Ret. Col. George Maracek, a 69-year-old former Green Beret who served in Korea and Vietnam, has been charged with the 1991 murder of his Thai wife Viparet. He maintains that he is innocent.

programme.title: 48 Hours: Sweetheart Murders
programme.desc: The story of sweethearts Sabrina Gonsalves and John Riggins who were brutally murdered in Davis, California, in 1980. Initially, police suspected Gerald Gallego, but he was in jail at the time of the murders.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: The Chi-Town Showdown
programme.desc: Metal Victorian-era burial casket; 1870s glass fire extinguisher grenades; a mechanical 1896 handcrafted Chicago fire station alarm box.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Blazing Saddles
programme.desc: Ton and Allen head to a Hollywood auction and face a new competitor with a seemingly endless cash flow. After they uncover a triple barrel shotgun and an antique camel saddle, Allen confronts an old childhood fear.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Without a Chute
programme.desc: After a tough auction, things get worse when the shop's landlord wants to talk to the guys. Ton tries to negotiate with him while Allen has to put an experimental powered parachute to the test to save the day's profits.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Cashville, Tennessee
programme.desc: Ton and Allen hit the road for Nashville, where they rumble with a band of tough, high-bidding bikers. The day explodes when the guys discover a 37 millimeter anti-tank gun and a cowboy's rodeo saddle.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Carolyn Goes Topless
programme.desc: A friendly competition with former employee Carolyn turns into a vicious battle for a 1968 Corvette Stingray, giving Carolyn the last laugh. Allen tests his Ninja skills with an antique sickle weapon.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Little-Ton Jones
programme.desc: Allen & Ton try to get the inside scoop on their competition by chatting up local auction buyers. The guys find some heavy gold-mining machinery from the 1950s & an amateur rocket that can travel faster than the speed of sound!

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Win Lose or Joust
programme.desc: Ton and Allen race back from an auction to purchase a rare comic valued at over $100,000. The guys uncover a Samurai sword used for ritual suicide and Allen lives out his childhood fantasy of becoming knight.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Gone in 20 Seconds
programme.desc: Ton and Allen get taken down by their fiercest opponent, CJ, but a twist of fate keeps the guys in the game. They uncover a Geiger counter and NASCAR impact wrenches, while their accountant delivers some shocking news.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Animal Instincts
programme.desc: The first pump action shotgun - a Winchester Model 1897; a hunter's collection of prize big game, full-body taxidermy.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Don't Taze Me Bro
programme.desc: Two fierce opponents best Ton and Allen at the auction, leaving the duo in the lurch with a "trash unit" and little chance of breaking even for the day. And Ton gets tazed.

programme.title: Auction Hunters: Ton-Geons & Dragons
programme.desc: While Ton soups up their new truck, Allen heads to an auction solo and encounters Carolyn and CJ. There he discovers LARP weaponry, a sword collection, and falconry gear, before Ton reveals their new ride: Betty.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Metal on Metal
programme.desc: A athlete is gunned down just weeks before starting college, leaving family heartbroken and detectives stumped. The man seems an unlikely target, until Lt. Joe Kenda pries into his personal life.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: My Worst Fear
programme.desc: Police are shocked to find a 13-year-old boy killed in an explosion, but their grief turns to alarm when they discover a second, unexploded bomb.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Victim Zero
programme.desc: A brawl erupts at a local bar ends with the shooting death of a army solider. To unravel the murder, Lt. Joe Kenda most infiltrate a revered military institution, and expose a dangerous vendetta.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: I Will Hunt You Down
programme.desc: When two escaped inmates surface in Colorado Springs, it’s up to Lt. Joe Kenda and his Fugitive Unit to bring them to justice.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: The Line Goes Dead
programme.desc: When the severed head of a wife and mother is found, Lt. Joe Kenda uses forensics and interrogation to find both her body and her killer.

programme.title: Homicide Hunter: Shallow Grave
programme.desc: Lt. Joe Kenda and his team investigate the scene of an apparent suicide, only to be forced to flee when they discover the house is booby-trapped.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
programme.desc: Examining the murder of former beauty queen Allison Baden-Clay, whose seemingly perfect life was riddled with secrets and horrors.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
programme.desc: In December 1987, Frank Vitkovic kills nine people in Melbourne's Australia Post headquarters. The day becomes a milestone along the road to stricter gun controls in Australia.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
programme.desc: In the Belanglo State Forest, in November 2010, Matthew Milat kills a school friend in cold blood. In doing so, he satisfies his desire to follow in the footsteps of his notorious uncle, Ivan.

programme.title: Crimes That Shook Australia
programme.desc: Anu Singh informs her friends of a suicide pact made between her and Joe Cinque, her boyfriend, but only Joe winds up dead, with toxicology reports revealing high levels of Rohypnol and heroin in his system.

programme.title: Killers: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: An examination of how Martika Ferguson and Tiffiney Crawford were killed in the US, uncovering the impact of a desire for petty revenge and a murder disguised as suicide.

programme.title: Killers: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: In the US, four parents were shot by Antoine Suggs. In the UK, Alice Ruggles was stalked and murdered by ex-partner, raising issues around coercive control.

programme.title: Crime Stories: The Missing Mom
programme.desc: Stephen Grant reported his wife, Tara Lynn Grant, missing five days after he said she left their house in an unidentified black car.

programme.title: Crime Stories: The Homicidal Handyman
programme.desc: In Sacramento in the mid 1980s, a ruthless killer was making his way through the fringe community of Oak Park.

programme.title: Crime Stories: The Happy Face Killer
programme.desc: A common-law couple was found guilty of the 1990 rape and murder of Taunja Bennett near Portland, Oregon.

programme.title: Crime Stories: The Abducted Atheists
programme.desc: American Atheist Madalyn Murray O'Hair and her family vanished in August 1995, leaving behind an ominous note.

programme.title: Crime Stories: A Way with Murder
programme.desc: Nancy, David and Susan Spangler are found shot to death in their home - the brutal slaughter is deemed a murder suicide.

programme.title: Bloodline Detectives
programme.desc: In October 1970, in Sacramento, California, 28-year-old court reporter Nancy Bennallack fails to show up for work, leaving colleagues worried and someone to find her body stabbed to death in her home.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: A woman poisoned her husband and others but was caught when an algicide enabled the police to prove her guilt.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: Evidence reveals a teacher who complained of being stalked and harassed by another teacher was scamming police.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: A man kills two people with bombs in an effort to get investors off his back and is sentenced to life with no chance of parole.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: A man arrested for the sexual assault and attempted murder of a boy in a European country is linked to a murder in Zurich, Switzerland.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow runoff carry manure into Milwaukee's water supply, making 400,000 people ill.

programme.title: Medical Detectives
programme.desc: A series of prostitutes, murdered near Sacramento, California, were gagged and molested, their clothes cut in random patterns and their hair cut off.

programme.title: Medical Detectives

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Can radiation from electrical appliances, power lines, microwave ovens, and wireless technology such as cell phones, baby monitors, alter our DNA, and cause illnesses?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Alien Abductions
programme.desc: Richard speaks with two alleged alien abductees about their claims they were taken aboard an alien space craft.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: For centuries, farmers have been discovering strange, complex geometric patterns comprised of rings, circles and other intricate shapes in their fields.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett
programme.desc: Richard meets the owners of an abnormal humanoid skull allegedly discovered in Mexico in the 1930s and a researcher who has had the skull subjected to numerous tests including carbon-14 dating, and DNA in order to determine it’s origin.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Mystery of Oak Island
programme.desc: Richard investigates Canada's best-known mysteries, the Oak Island treasure. Also known as the money pit, the site has become one of the world's deepest and most costly archaeological digs.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: Energy Healing
programme.desc: Several energy healers claim they are able to produce spontaneous miraculous healing through a laying on of hands. Claims of similar such healing by a man in a remote village in Brazil, known as John of God are also examined.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Execution of MLK and The Framing of James Earl Ray
programme.desc: In this re-examination of the assassination of civil rights leader Martin Luther King, Richard meets several researchers who provide evidence that the convicted gunman, James Earl Ray was not the real triggerman.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Birthers - Obama's Birth Certificate
programme.desc: Richard interviews several prominent 'Birthers' who present evidence that proves Barack Obama is not eligible to be President because he does not meet the qualifications of a natural born citizen.

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: RFID Chips
programme.desc: Could implantable RFID chips or Radio-frequency identification tags currently in widespread use in retail industry be used to surreptitiously track and control people?

programme.title: The Conspiracy Show With Richard Syrett: The Assassination of John Lennon
programme.desc: The Conspiracy Show travels to New York, LA and Toronto in order to investigate the murder of John Lennon. Several researchers posit that Lennon's supposed killer Mark David Chapman was either a patsy or a mind-controlled assassin.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Metropolitan Museum, New York
programme.desc: From the mystery of a king’s madness to a silver chalice some think is the Holy Grail, discover the secrets inside the Metropolitan Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the National Archeological Museum of Athens
programme.desc: From a dodgy archeologist who discovers a golden mask to an ancient gadget that reveals the future, discover the secrets inside the National Archaeological Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the State Hermitage Museum
programme.desc: From ancient mummies who sport tattoos to re-examining who really killed Rasputin, discover the secrets inside the State Hermitage Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the Topkapi Palace Museum, Istanbul
programme.desc: From assassins who plot to poison a Sultan to bitter enemies who sign the world’s first peace treaty, discover the secrets inside the Topkapi Palace Museum.

programme.title: Museum Secrets: Inside the American Museum of Natural History, New York
programme.desc: From a dinosaur embryo that solves a murder mystery to a spacecraft catching a comet’s tail, discover the secrets inside the American Museum of Natural History.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Under Pressure
programme.desc: After submarine exploration of Grand Cayman Island, George dives with Canadian Armed Forces' experimental deep pressure unit. With explorers Chris Harvey-Clark and Phil Nuytten he investigates newly discovered "Black Smoker" deep sea vents.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Costa Rica
programme.desc: George explores the volcanoes of Costa Rica with an international team of scientists, rafts down white water rivers in full flood, rappels down raging waterfalls, and handles some of deadliest snakes this tropical country has to offer.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Iceland: Fire and Ice
programme.desc: George meets President of Iceland Ólafur Grímsson who spearheaded use of geothermal power; visits Blue Lagoon and geysers; explores volcanically active Haimaey Island and climbs an iceberg calving off the glaciers at Vatnajokull Lagoon.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Avalanches
programme.desc: Whenever there is unstable snow and gravity, gravity always wins. George travels to the Rocky Mountains to learn about how to stay alive in the back country.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Tornadoes
programme.desc: George embarks on his annual pilgrimage to the "Tornado Alley" area of the central U.S. to guide groups of people who want to witness the brute force of tornadoes in action

programme.title: Angry Planet: Midwest Stormfest
programme.desc: This season the tornadoes begin early - in February. Through the season, George follows the big weather from Missouri west to the Dakotas, and dodges the huge, destructive Greensburg tornado in southern Kansas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hurricane Triple Threat
programme.desc: George chases deadly hurricanes in the USA: Gustav in Louisiana and Hanna in North Carolina. He then flies with USAF Hurricane Hunters into Ike over Cuba, meeting it again as it makes a wild destructive landfall in Galveston, Texas.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Hawaii: Surf & Snow
programme.desc: In Hawaii George explores the wild side: diving with Galapagos sharks, ascending to snow-covered Mauna Kea, dental cleaning by Peppermint Cleaner Shrimp, extreme surfing at Banzai Pipeline, getting his boots lit on fire by Kilauea volcano.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Arctic Winter
programme.desc: George links up with two experienced polar adventurers to explore frigid Canadian Arctic winter. The team uses skis to cross the fractured sea ice of Frobisher Bay, then dog teams to head North through the spectacular Auyuittuq Mountains.

programme.title: Angry Planet: Island Caving
programme.desc: Accompanied by a group of hardcore cavers from Alberta and British Columbia, George descends into spectacular caves that lead deep into the earth on Vancouver Island. The team carries inflatable kayaks to explore a dark underground river.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Superscopes
programme.desc: The human eye can only see visible light, a small spectrum of electromagnetic waves. Today, we have instruments that let us perceive all wavelengths.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Fermi Paradox
programme.desc: The Fermi Paradox asks the ultimate question: Given a few reasonable assumptions about the chance of life arising elsewhere in the universe and the distance between the stars, why has an alien civilization not presented itself?

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Cosmic Rainbow
programme.desc: In the world of astronomy, how do we overcome the limits imposed by our own eyesight? The universe comes alive by viewing the cosmos in infrared light to gamma radiation.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas
programme.desc: See the sun, moon, planets, and galaxies through the eyes of intrepid machines that are blazing a trail across the solar system. A gateway to bold adventures, these cameras are delivering spectacular new cosmic vistas.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Night Fire
programme.desc: The Northern Lights are more than just beautiful; they tell us about the make up of our planet, our sun, and may even be a signpost for the possibility of life on other planets.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: The Hot Zone
programme.desc: Mercury is one Earth's closest neighbors, but for centuries it eluded astronomer's best efforts to learn its secrets. But that has changed thanks to missions like Mariner and most recently, Messenger.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas
programme.desc: See the sun, moon, planets, and galaxies through the eyes of intrepid machines that are blazing a trail across the solar system. A gateway to bold adventures, these cameras are delivering spectacular new cosmic vistas.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Landing on Mars
programme.desc: Mars sparks our curiosity like no other planet because it is so reachable. Despite having landed there, most of the information we gather is still done by instruments in the planet's orbit.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Water Worlds
programme.desc: The key ingredient to life in the universe is water. Until recently we thought Earth was the only place in the solar system to have any water at all. Now that probes are peering around nearby worlds we are finding that is just not the case.

programme.title: Cosmic Vistas: Hubble's Renaissance
programme.desc: Before we can reach space, we must first be able to see it. Hundreds of years ago, the simple telescope was used to give us a seemingly closer vantage. Today, we use the awe-inspiring, and super powerful, Hubble Space Telescope.

programme.title: Wildest Islands

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Island of the Devil

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: One for the Money
programme.desc: It hurts Dog to see people who after years of walking the straight and narrow, suddenly lose their way. But today's case is a member of the law enforcement community who has recently gone astray, and that truly troubles the Dog.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Reality Check
programme.desc: Dog is back in Denver and in hot pursuit of a woman wanted for identity theft. The sad reality is that the identify which has been robbed is her own. She's traded in a bright future for a life on the wrong side of the law.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: No Piece of Cake
programme.desc: Being hunted by the Dog is a lousy way to celebrate your birthday but that's exactly the spot that Robert finds himself in today. He's missed his court date, quit his job, and vacated his last known address.

programme.title: Dog The Bounty Hunter: Prayers and Mace
programme.desc: Both brother Tim and fellow Honolulu bondsman James need Dog's help to track down their hard to find fugitives. The two cases on the docket today are challenging, especially the hunt for Gregory, a felon with a lengthy rap sheet.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Fowl Playhouse
programme.desc: When Jase finds out Jep is planning to buy his kids a fancy playhouse from a catalogue, he and the guys decide to build one for Jep themselves. But the playhouse ends up looking more like a military-style duck blind.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: The Big LeCOWski
programme.desc: When Sadie spends the day at Duck Commander for a school project, the guys try to show her the ins-and-outs of how a successful company works. But when Willie questions Jase's ingenuity in creating new products they get into a debate.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Duck Dynasty
programme.desc: The Robertson family pulls together a family reunion to give Mia a big send-off before her surgery.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Governor's Travels
programme.desc: When the Governor of Louisiana announces that he will be coming to Duck Commander to present Willie with a commerce award, John Luke volunteers to give the introduction speech.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack and Gown
programme.desc: With Reed's graduation day approaching, Jase decides to take Reed frog hunting the night before, like he did on the day of his own graduation. Willie and Si join in, and Jase tries to keep them from imparting their "college advice" on Reed.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Hands on a Woodchipper
programme.desc: When Willie agrees to sponsor Mountain Man's radio contest, a "Hands on a Hardbody" competition, he somehow finds himself competing against Si for Mountain Man's last minute prize: a woodchipper.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Quack Draft
programme.desc: When the Robertsons participate in the West Monroe Fire Department pancake breakfast, the firemen invite Willie and his brothers to train with the department for the day. Meanwhile, Phil and Si accompany Miss Kay on a trip to visit a family grave.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Brand of Brothers
programme.desc: When Willie contemplates re-branding the Duck Commander image, Jase takes issue with having an outsider design their image, so he and the guys create their own new logo and business cards. Meanwhile, Willie and Phil help Bella with her science project.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: De-Bug Life
programme.desc: The guys realize it is time for the annual cleaning of the duck blinds when they come across a gator using one as a home. Meanwhile, while the kids are running the church car wash, Si and Willie disagree on the best way to draw in customers.

programme.title: Duck Dynasty: Men vs. Wild
programme.desc: Brotherly competition is taken to a whole new level when Jase and Willie challenge Alan and Jep to a survival race through the woods on Phil's property. Meanwhile, Si is interested in finding a new hobby when he sees Kay cutting coupons.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Silent Bell
programme.desc: A pastor must follow a church council's decision to fire most of the staff at the school because they're not Christian.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Reunion
programme.desc: Mark attends his high school class reunion and learns that some memories are bittersweet for his old friends.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: The Source
programme.desc: A teenage girl tells school reporters that a driver came on to her right before the bus got into an accident. She may not be telling the truth. Jonathan tries to teach the news writers the correct way to report a story.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: The problems of the handicapped are addressed when an amputee joins a crusade after a false fire alarm.

programme.title: Highway to Heaven: Goodbye, Mr. Zelinka
programme.desc: Mr. Zelinka is a beloved teacher who is being forced to retire because he's old. Jonathan and Mark know that he would prefer to continue teaching.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Big Bend National Park
programme.desc: Big Bend National Park: A series of incidents in Big Bend National Park leave experts convinced of secret alien activities taking place there.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Death Valley National Park
programme.desc: Death Valley National Park: Discover the forces living in the secret underworld of Death Valley National Park, where a series of deaths and disappearances have taken place.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Cleveland National Forest
programme.desc: Cleveland National Forest: Explore Cleveland National Forest, where reports of gun-wielding ghosts, savage creatures and otherworldly portals are rife.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Apache Sitgreaves National Forests
programme.desc: Apache-Sitgreaves National Forest: A trip through the ginormous forest in Arizona. Strange encounters rumoured to have happened there include an alien abduction.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Great Smokey Mountains National Park
programme.desc: Great Smokey Mountains National Park: A stroll through a national park that is the alleged underground base for a powerful secret society.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Mark Twain National Forest
programme.desc: Mark Twain National Forest: Some claim that the dark, mysterious energies that surround this national park point to treasures from a lost civilisation that are hidden there.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Chaco Culture National Historical Park
programme.desc: Was Chaco Canyon a complex ceremonial center with extraordinary astronomical alignments of buildings and roads?

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Yosemite National Park
programme.desc: One of America’s most beloved wilderness areas, Yosemite National Park, has a dark side – ghost and UFO sightings, mysterious disappearances and a pre-historic Pterodactyl - which experts believe is caused by a rift in the fabric of time.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Great Sand Dunes National Park
programme.desc: Great Sand Dunes National Park: A stroll through Great Sand Dunes National Park, where strange lights, animal mutilations and other bizarre incidents have been spotted.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Lassen Volcanic National Park
programme.desc: Lassen Volcanic National Park: Reports of sightings here include reptiles - known as reptoids - who work with aliens and can appear in human form.

programme.title: National Park Mysteries: Crater Lake National Park
programme.desc: The tourist-attracting waters of Crater Lake National Park may also be the epicenter of dark and sinister forces from Native American lore that lure visitors to their deaths.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Fragment
programme.desc: Claudia is hospitalized after the shooting incident; Krystle is implicated. Logan Rhinewood continues to amass Devner-Carrington; Cecil proposes marriage to Alexis.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Shakedown
programme.desc: Claudia remains confused following the accident; Jeff urges Fallon to reconcile; Steven is blackmailed by a young drifter.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Two Princes
programme.desc: Steven is arrested; Alexis and Cecil plan to marry; Fallon tells Nick it's over between them; Claudia believes Fallon's baby is her now deceased daughter Lindsay.

programme.title: Dynasty: Cliffs
programme.desc: Middle Eastern oilman Farouk, anxious for vengeance against Blake, tells Nick that Carrington is responsible for the death of his brother. Nick attacks Blake, who is left unconscious at the bottom of a deep ravin.

programme.title: Dynasty: The Plea
programme.desc: Krystle finds Blake in the mountains and gets him to the hospital. Nick Toscanni disappears. Cecil is also brought to the hospital, and he's very weak. A police investigation is started to find the baby.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: D-Day (June 6,1944)
programme.desc: D-Day, the 6th of June--one of the most pivotal moments in world history. In a massive armada of thousands of ships, 3,000,000 allied troops land at Three Beaches in Normandy, France.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Retaking France
programme.desc: Six weeks after the D-Day invasion of the Beaches of Normandy, the allied forces are pushing the Germans back through the French countryside. Town by town is won back as Panzer divisions retreat back to the Meuse River.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Operation Dragoon (August 1944)
programme.desc: The Allies break into France and push the Germans back to their own border.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Liberation of the Paris
programme.desc: Both political and military expediency were factors in the return of the French capital to its people.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Arnhem
programme.desc: The Allies launch an attack designed to push the Germans back to the Rhine River.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory
programme.desc: Gen. Douglas MacArthur's return to the Philippines, and the final push to retake the islands after two years of Japanese occupation.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: The Last Stronghold (November 1944)
programme.desc: American B-29 high-altitude bomber attacks Japan near the end of World War II.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Mount Belvedere
programme.desc: The Battle of the Gothic Line in 1944.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Battle of the Bulge (December 1944)
programme.desc: The December 1944 battle pitting American forces against German Panzer divisions in the Ardennes Forest.

programme.title: Battlefront: WWII Road To Victory: Mount Hot Rocks (February 19, 1945)
programme.desc: American forces landing on the shores of Iwo Jima encounter Japanese combatants willing to fight to the death.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Ashley & Mike
programme.desc: For over seven years, Ashley has sent dramatically altered photos of herself to her soul mate, Mike, to make her look thinner and more attractive to him.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Aaliyah & Alicia
programme.desc: Aaliyah has been able to share her life struggles with Alicia. However, despite living only 70 blocks away, Alicia refuses to meet her as she hasn't come out to her friends and family yet.

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Nick & Melissa
programme.desc: Nick and Melissa fell in love at age 12 when they met on an online chat room. Now 18, Nick is ready to meet Melissa. But is Melissa the real deal or has Nick been entangled in lies?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Keyonnah & Bow Wow
programme.desc: Superfan Keyonnah can hardly believe her luck, she's almost certain that she's been having an online relationship with her childhood crush, the rapper Bow Wow. Is this too good to be true?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Mike & Caroline
programme.desc: Mike fell in love with Caroline online. He grew suspicious when she continually evaded meeting him, yet left notes at his car. Is Caroline an imposter or is she just afraid?

programme.title: Catfish: The TV Show: Craig & Zoe
programme.desc: Craig's sister introduced him to a beautiful girl named Zoe on Facebook. After talks online of a future life together, things turn sour. Now he wants to know who he gave his heart to.

programme.title: Friendzone: Friendzone 111
programme.desc: Stephanie is tired of only fantasizing about Mike's "sexy face" and she's ready to make him her boyfriend. Sean and Kate were friends at first sight, now Sean wants to up the stakes.

programme.title: Friendzone: Friendzone 106
programme.desc: Jaime has only known Steve for a year but the feelings she has for him make her want to be more than friends. Together Jackie and Frank have lost almost 200 pounds, newly fit Jackie now wants to make Frank her boyfriend.

programme.title: Friendzone: Friendzone 207
programme.desc: Amy tells her study partner and best friend that she's in love. Tonee wants to come clean with his feelings for his best friend Ryan.

programme.title: Friendzone: Friendzone 102
programme.desc: Heather must convince her best friend Gary that she's more than "one of the guys", while Kevin tries to prove to Janine that nice guys finish first.

programme.title: The Hills: Drama Follows Them / Better Off As Friends
programme.desc: Lauren runs into Spencer at Stephanie's birthday, and the birthday girl is torn between her brother and friend. Someone is fired at People's Revolution, and Whitney must step up. (S4, ep2)

programme.title: The Hills: Boys Make Girls Cry / Something Has To Change
programme.desc: A trip to Vegas breaks down when Audrina snubs Lauren. Brody brings up Stephanie's past, and the boys end up in jail. Heidi's sister wants to move in, but Spencer wants her out. (S4, ep4)

programme.title: The Hills: You Always Miss a Best Friend / When Lauren's Away...
programme.desc: Heidi's sister Holly asks Lauren and Heidi to reconsider what happened to their friendship. Audrina and Justin's relationship is tested as she works a huge event for a record company. (S4, ep6)

programme.title: The Hills: Don't Act Innocent / If She Never Met Spencer...
programme.desc: The truth comes out about Stephanie's relationship with Lauren's ex, Doug, when they all meet up for the first time at his pool party. Heidi's mother surprises her in LA. (S4, ep8)

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Elixir
programme.desc: The Impossible Missions Force offers a "fountain of youth" to the vain and dictatorial widow (guest star Ruth Roman) of the late president of a small South American country in order to depose her.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Exchange
programme.desc: Cinnamon is captured and the enemy discovers the one thing that would unmask her identity and expose the Impossible Mission Force.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Bargain
programme.desc: The former dictator of a Caribbean island is made to have hallucinatory visions in which he sees his ominous destiny as part of an ingenious plot designed by the Impossible Missions Force.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Mind of Stefan Miklos
programme.desc: The Impossible Missions Force is pitted against the ingenious logic of an enemy agent (guest Steve Ihnat) as the IMF attempts to lead the agent through a maze of complicated clues in order to reach a false conclusion.

programme.title: Mission: Impossible: The Freeze
programme.desc: Before a statute of limitations expires on a 10-million dollar robbery, the Impossible Missions Force must spring the suspect from prison and dupe him into leading police to the hiding place of the money.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: So much to lose
programme.desc: Leah breaks down during a doctor's visit, Chelsea starts hanging out with her ex again, Kailyn starts dating a new guy, and Jenelle convinces her mum to let her come home.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Change of Heart
programme.desc: Jo's parents give Kailyn an ultimatum, Jenelle has a change of heart, Leah and Corey give it another shot, and Chelsea tells Megan she's giving Adam another chance.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Moving In, Moving On
programme.desc: Kailyn is forced to borrow money from Jo, Chelsea tells Meghan that she's going to let Adam move in, Jenelle meets a new guy, and Leah and Corey decide to move in together.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Too Much Too Fast
programme.desc: Jenelle breaks Barbara's rules again, things get tough for Corey and Leah, Chelsea tries to keep something from her dad, and Jo's parents kick Kailyn and Isaac out.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Taking Sides
programme.desc: Things between Adam and Megan reach a boiling point, Barbara helps Jenelle get her financial aid, things improve between Leah and Corey and Kailyn moves back in with Jo's parents.

programme.title: Teen Mom 2: Switching Gears
programme.desc: Corey surprises Leah with a proposal, Chelsea shifts her focus back to school, Kailyn gets a second job and Jenelle wears out her welcome at a friend's house.

programme.title: Beyond The Myth: The SS Unveiled: Unrepentant: The SS And Its Myth After 1945
programme.desc: Unrepentant: The SS and Its Myth After 1945: Drawing a line to the present, the atrocities of the SS are beyond all doubt, yet, there is a cult surrounding 'old comrades' today.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: The Lost Continent (Atlantis)
programme.desc: As the Nazis rose to power, Himmler and the SS were convinced Atlantis could be found, and instigated a series of archeological expeditions seeking uncover and exploit its secrets.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: Walking with Witches
programme.desc: Convinced the medieval witch trials were a Judeo-Christian attempt to exterminate the Aryans, Himmler established a special team led by Reinhard Heydrich to build a database of medieval German witchcraft practices.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: The Quest for the Holy Grail
programme.desc: Revealing the work of a secret team of scientists and scholars established by SS leader Heinrich Himmler to scour the globe seeking the origins of the Aryan race and collect ancient artifacts.

programme.title: Secret Nazi Expeditions: Into the Mountains (Tibet)
programme.desc: Revealing the work of a secret team of scientists and scholars established by SS leader Heinrich Himmler to scour the globe seeking the origins of the Aryan race and collect ancient artifacts.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Go Fish!
programme.desc: The fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, battle for the elusive and lucrative giant Bluefin tuna, with harpooners going up against rod-and-reelers.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: The Numbers War
programme.desc: The fishermen of Gloucester battle for prime fishing spots in their quest to catch the elusive and lucrative Bluefin Tuna.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Fish Fight
programme.desc: For the tuna fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, it's tournament time, and, with a big cash prize up for grabs, the fight is on to catch the biggest bluefin.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Shark Attack
programme.desc: The fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, are facing a dangerous new foe, with great white sharks swarming into local waters.

programme.title: Wicked Tuna: Sweet Smell of Revenge
programme.desc: The fishermen of Gloucester, Massachusetts, battle droves of bumbling amateur fishermen in their bid to catch lucrative Atlantic Bluefin Tuna.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Rogers Pass
programme.desc: Montana Game Warden Bryan Golie helps out when a hunter is injured while in the woods. Elsewhere, Wardens chase down hunters that don't stop at their check station; with surprising results.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Elk Dummy
programme.desc: Montana game wardens deploy elk decoys across the state in hopes of catching would-be poachers.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Wolf Creek
programme.desc: Wardens patrol the Wolf Creek area of Montana for illegal hunting activity. Elsewhere, Wardens conduct a mule deer decoy operation.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Grizzly Bear
programme.desc: This week, game wardens deal with a missing child who was last seen dragged off by a bear... good thing it's only a training excercise. Meanwhile it's opening season for shed antler hunters and the wardens have their hands full!

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Water Rescue
programme.desc: This week, a couple of wardens face off with early season anglers over gaming laws while other wardens get trained in open water rescue techniques.

programme.title: Wardens: Operation Paddlefish
programme.desc: This week, wardens encounter a crowd after busting a couple of antler poachers, crack down on anglers who are cheating the system during paddlefish season, and one warden deals with a rowdy group of partying campers.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries: UFO Odyssey
programme.desc: This episode includes: UFO Odyssey, Reporter's Trucker and Haunted Mansion.

programme.title: 30 Insane UFO Encounters
programme.desc: A review of the 30 most insane UFO encounters for your eyes delight and its up to you to make your own mind up, if these amazing encounters could be the real deal or one big elaborate hoax

programme.title: Aliens In USSR
programme.desc: Unsolved Soviet Union Alien Mysteries: Dyatlov Pass incident, Petrozavodsk miracle, Voronezh case. Amaze and frighten, but doubt of alien involvement.

programme.title: Alien Agenda: Planet Earth
programme.desc: Alien Agenda Planet Earth reveals the shocking truth about UFOs and Extraterrestrials, a truth that has been suppressed and hidden for centuries.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 12
programme.desc: Sketch is monkeying around with Jenny, Joe's Gary Lineker tat gets the red card and Alice fixes Sannahs unfortunate inking error.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 9
programme.desc: Alice paints over Francesca's pitiful Picasso. Sketch gives Madison a memorial to her mum.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 4
programme.desc: John's tattoo is on another planet, while Alec has made a big mistake with a tiny inking.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 8

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Episode 4
programme.desc: Lisa pays tribute to her idol Robbie Williams, while Jack covers his literal leg tattoo.

programme.title: Geordie OGs

programme.desc: Marnie and Casey do a photo shoot for OK! Magazine. Holly takes a class in public speaking and it's date night for Gary and Emma. The stress of Aaron's upcoming fight is beginning to show. (S1, ep2)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie gives Casey a sexy birthday present. Holly faces her fears at Sussex Uni. Aaron meets up with Nathan and Sam to talk about mental health. Emma has a big surprise for Gary. (S1, ep3)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie shows off her baby bump in a magazine shoot. Gary plays golf with Chris Hughes. Aaron's shocked to find out who his opponent is in his MMA fight and Holly says a tearful goodbye. (S1, ep4)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Gary and Emma share some special news and Aaron gets stuck into training. Holly goes to the House of Lords and Marnie and Casey get a shock when an photo of Casey is sent to a newspaper. (S1, ep5)

programme.title: Geordie OGs
programme.desc: Marnie could give birth at any moment. Sophie opens up to Holly about her heartbreak. Chester gives Gary the run around and Aaron faces up to the reality of becoming a Dad. (S2, ep1)

programme.title: Wildest Latin America: Amazon: One Jungle, Many Worlds
programme.desc: The Amazon is the largest rainforest in the world, and the richest ecosystem on earth. This film uncovers what makes the Amazon such a powerhouse of evolution; how it has come to home a third of all species on the planet.

programme.title: Wildest Arctic: The Tundra: Desert of Ice
programme.desc: Covering a fifth of the earth's surface, the Arctic tundra is one of the coldest places on the planet. The piercing temperatures and powerful winds challenge even the hardiest of creatures.

programme.title: Australia's Wild Places: The Coast of the Whalesharks
programme.desc: On the remote coastline of Australia’s northwest, there are great natural wonders to be found. Above the sea on this sunbaked coast, there is very little; only a sparse landscape shaped by the arid conditions. But here, the miracle reveals itself…

programme.title: Wildest India: Ganges: River of Life
programme.desc: Then watch, the longest river in India, The Ganges. It's a lifeline to wild animals, from rhinoceros and river dolphins to otters and gharials.

programme.title: Wildest India: Thar Desert: Sacred Sands
programme.desc: Covering 200,000 km sq, India's Thar desert is one of the harshest places on Earth. Desiccating winds and near permanent drought has earned this unforgiving land another name: the region of death.

programme.title: Camp Lakebottom: Terror form the Toybox / Slimeball Run

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur Sells Out/Mind Your Manners
programme.desc: To earn the money for a new video game, Arthur decides to sell his old toys through his school's Web site. The Tibble Twins want to take Grandmother out to dinner, but she'd rather stay home. Is it because of their terrible table manners?

programme.title: Arthur: Arthur's Dummy Disaster/Francine and The Feline
programme.desc: When painfully shy George brings a ventriloquist dummy to school, a popular kid is born! Then, Arthur thinks the best pet to have is a dog, and Francine probably would have agreed--that is, until little Nemo, a cute kitten, came along.

programme.title: Arthur: Attack Of The Turbo Tibbles/D.W. Tricks The Tooth Fairy
programme.desc: If you think the Tibbles are bad now, imagine what they'd be like if they had super powers! Then, D.W. needs money, quick. When she tries to pull a fast one on the Tooth Fairy, will Arthur have to pay?

programme.title: Arthur: Based on a True Story (Part 1 & 2)
programme.desc: There's a new kid in town: Ladonna Compson, the Louisiana native with the gift of gab. Ladonna charms Arthur and friends with her amazing "true" stories. But are her stories really fact…or fiction?

programme.title: Arthur: Popular Girls/Buster's Growing Grudge
programme.desc: Sue Ellen and Fern take a popularity quiz in a magazine and change their personalities to become more likable, but it backfires. And Buster's convinced that his great joke will be the difference between a successful oral report and failure.

programme.title: Arthur: Phony Fern/Brain's Shocking Secret
programme.desc: When Fern's parents give her a cell phone for emergencies, she soon becomes hooked on it. Brain is the smartest kid in class. Everyone knows that! What they don't know is that he was kept back in Kindergarten. Will Binky spill the beans?

programme.title: Arthur: Binky Vs. Binky/Operation: DW!
programme.desc: Binky may be tough and strong, but when it comes to sports, he always seems to choke. D.W. is having trouble hearing and needs an ear operation. Is it okay to be just a little bit scared?

programme.title: Arthur: Paradise Lost/The Pride of Lakewood
programme.desc: Baby Kate is growing up! But strangely enough, the more words she learns, the less she seems to understand their dog. To boost school spirit, Arthur and his classmates form the Lakewood Pride Committee, but not everyone wants to join.

programme.title: Arthur: Sleep No More/Pet Peeved
programme.desc: Buster is convinced that Pizza Paula's all-you-can-eat contest is his destiny. But how can he possibly win such an important contest without any sleep? Binky volunteers to look after Francine's cat, Nemo, for the weekend.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Easy as ABC
programme.desc: When Baby Bop uses the caboose mailbox to deliver a special invitation to her tea party, she discovers that no one can read her handwriting. Barney and his friends find creative ways to help her learn her ABCs!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Gift Of The Dinos - Winter
programme.desc: It's Christmas and BJ dreams of getting a new baseball glove to go with his baseball, while Riff hopes for new drumsticks to use his drum. Barney reminds them that Christmas is more than just getting gifts and helps them learn the meaning of Christmas.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: A Perfectly Purple Day
programme.desc: When Sarah accidentally spills red paint into the blue paint, a mess turns into a perfectly purple day. The children paint pictures, make purple flowers and plan a purple party for their friend Colleen.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Day and Night
programme.desc: With a little Barney magic, the children experience the differences between day and night!

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Rabbits - Listen
programme.desc: BJ is pretending to be a great detective. But all great detectives need a mystery to solve! One quickly comes his way when an animal nibbles on one of Baby Bop's healthy snacks.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Let Your Creativity Fly!
programme.desc: After finding Barney's paper airplane in the park and pretending to fly like airplanes themselves, Baby Bop and BJ are inspired to use their imaginations. Baby Bop teaches a special dance and BJ pretends to be Captain Pickles.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: Sharing Is Caring!
programme.desc: The children bring toys to donate to Mr. Boyd's toy drive. Baby Bop and BJ arrive and after some convincing, Baby Bop learns about the merits of sharing.

programme.title: Barney and Friends: On the Road Again
programme.desc: Barney and his friends have a "wheel good fun" day learning about cars. Barney creates a colorful, kid-size car, the dinos learn about seatbelt safety, and everyone discovers there are many different styles of cars.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Landmine
programme.desc: Landmine tries to adjust to life on Earth while recovering from the injury Starscream's null cannons inflicted on him.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Space

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Rush

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Speed
programme.desc: Starscream plots how he can defend his title as Megatron's right-hand man while Thundercracker lures the Autobots into a trap. Meanwhile, Hot Shot tries another attempt to beat Override.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Collapse
programme.desc: Megatron searches for the Cyber Planet Key with extreme force, while the Autobots try to stop the massive earthquakes that are threatening the city.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Time
programme.desc: Red Alert becomes concerned at the desperate measures Hot Shot is taking to beat Override, while Vector Prime and the kids travel to the Sahara in search of the Omega Lock.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Search
programme.desc: The Autobots join Vector Prime to search for the Omega Lock, while Overhaul is left stranded by a surprise attack by Megatron and Starscream.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Deep
programme.desc: Overhaul and Megatron try to convince inhabitants of the Jungle Planet of their 'good' intentions, while the Autobots investigate the appearance of a new Atlantean symbol.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Ship
programme.desc: After the "Atlantis" ship disappeared, Optimus Prime and Megatron head to the moon where it's hiding while Vector Prime and the kids locate the Omega Lock.

programme.title: Transformers: Cybertron: Hero
programme.desc: Optimus discovers that Hot Shot has disobeyed his orders while Red Alert has covered for him. However, he is forced to put his trust into Hot Shot to win the Cyber Key from Override.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Samira & Shawn
programme.desc: Busy professionals Samira and Shawn are living out of a suitcase as they hunt for the perfect house-ideally a luxury pad close to Shawn's office. The Property Brothers help them find a budget-friendly dream home.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Angie & Tito
programme.desc: Angie and Tito live in a cramped apartment full of their kid's toys. They want a large, modern house they can grow into, close to downtown, with a gourmet kitchen and lots of closets. The Property Brothers come to help.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Matt & Krysten
programme.desc: Matt and Krysten are trading in their condo for a large modern house where they can put down roots. The Property Brothers come to the rescue helping them find a budget-friendly dream home that can be renovated on time.

programme.title: Property Brothers: James & David
programme.desc: James and David are desperate to escape their nightmare townhouse, the one with mountainous stairs beside a stinky sewage treatment plant. The Property Brothers come to the rescue helping them find a dream home.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Parker & Francesca
programme.desc: Parker and Francesca live on top of each other in a tiny condo overlooking a concrete jungle. The Property Brothers come to the rescue helping them find a budget-friendly dream home that can be renovated on time.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Caitlin & Steve
programme.desc: Having sold their downtown condo, Caitlin and Steve are wearing out their welcome with friends in the ‘burbs. They want a big, open concept, eco-home within walking distance of all amenities.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Beneath These Streets
programme.desc: While the Turtles, in disguise, are at a movie, Shredder interrupts a press conference to steal a new medi-laser.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles on Trial
programme.desc: While the Turtles go on a talk show to prove their innocence to the world, Krang steals a robot and some Solarbenite from a military warehouse, with the intention of destroying the Turtles himself.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Attack of the 50-Foot Irma
programme.desc: While the Turtles are planning Irma's birthday party, Shredder steals an Exaboron Isotope from a meteorite crash. Krang creates an Exaboron-powered Enlarging Ray which causes six times normal growth, with the intention of using it on Shredder.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Maltese Hamster
programme.desc: Shredder gives futuristic laser weapons to mobster Tony Vivaldi and his underlings. Shredder wants to find the Maltese Hamster statue of him, which has inside it the formula for ultimate fuel for the Technodrome.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Sky Turtles
programme.desc: Krang creates a Gravity Altering Device which causes everyone in the city, including the Turtles, to start floating.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Old Switcheroo
programme.desc: Two huge electrodes at Cybertech Labs produce a startling effect on two long-time foes. The mind of the Turtles master Splinter winds up in the body of the evil Shredder - and vice versa, causing confusion among their associates.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Burne's Blues
programme.desc: Burne is out to prove to April that he can still dig in the trenches and find good stories after deriding her for failing to come up with anything good lately.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): The Fifth Turtle
programme.desc: On the occasion of a rare conjunction of the Earth, Moon, and Stars, Shredder steals some Capsidium Crystals.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Enter: The Rat King
programme.desc: April does a story on the rat problem the city is facing, and the Rat King kidnaps her. The Rat King is the leader of all the rats under his form of government known as the Ratocracy.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): Turtles at the Earth's Core
programme.desc: The Turtles venture down to the Earth's core and discover a lost prehistoric world complete with dinosaurs.

programme.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1987): April Foolish
programme.desc: Emperor Aleister and his daughter Princess Mallory arrive from the small kingdom of Malacuria where there has been a recent discovery of a vast deposit of Lydium - 90, the most powerful source of energy found on Earth.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies pop up from behind the hill and rush off to see a little Indian boy playing on a drum called a Tabla. In Teletubbyland, it's time for Tubby Toast.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, some voice trumpets rise and make some funny animal noises. They baa like sheep, moo like cows, meow like cats and oink like pigs.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: A voice trumpet rises and Laa-Laa and Dipsy play a going up and going down game. They go up the hill and then they go down the hill. They do it again and again.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch a little girl whose Mum has a very special job. Po has too much Tubby Custard and Tubby Custard Tummy and there's time foe one more dance before Tubby Bye-Bye.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po fetches the other to watch Andy Brown and two children look for some rabbits. In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky is looking for Po.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: In Teletubbyland, Tinky Winky was asleep, Dipsy was making some adjustments and Laa-Laa was eating Tubby Toast. But nobody knew what Po was doing.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Po say's "Eh-oh" and runs off to join the other Tubbies when they watch Andy Brown and some children go on a boat trip to feed some sealions. The Tubbies are very busy at the switch panel, pressing buttons and pushing levers.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Some voice trumpets rise to wish the Tubbies good morning. The Teletubbies watch some children herding sheep. The Teletubbies go in and out, and up and down, round the corner and along to top until it is time for Tubby Custard.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies watch as Andy Brown shows some children butterflies. The Teletubbies love to fly their kites and a strong wind blows Po all over Teletubbyland.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies meet to watch some children have great fun watering the garden. The Teletubbies play a hiding game.

programme.title: Race to the Finish
programme.desc: Mayor Bentley announces that Sunflower Valley is to stage its very first athletics tournament. A team from Sunflower Valley is to compete against a team of visiting athletes. Plus there is to be a special machine event!

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Robert and Dot, Bob’s Mum and Dad are visiting the Valley. Bob shows them around, pointing out their recycling storeroom. Robert notices Bob’s old rubble chute. Bob explains that he hasn’t had much use for it out in the valley.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is erecting a tent to accommodate some newcomers to the valley. Mr Bentley is organising everything, but unfortunately leaves his phone with Spud who promptly declares himself ‘Mr Bentley’s Assistant’.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has been asked to install a new air conditioning unit at Mr Sabatini’s restaurant. Wendy and Molly meanwhile have been asked to redecorate the library at the school.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Farmer Pickles wants a fence built around his compost heap, as Scruffty keeps getting into it. Muck is excited at the prospect of such a mucky job. JJ sends Trix up to the farm with the materials.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is putting up some new signs in Sunflower Valley to help people find their way round the valley. Meanwhile Dizzy has been asked by Ela Stevenson if she’ll be the ‘walking bus’ and help the children get to school.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob’s just finished tidying his mobile home when Farmer Pickles comes and asks if he’ll help with his Sunflower Harvest. He needs a silo built in which to store the ripe sunflowers before they’re taken to the factory.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and Wendy are converting an old shop into a brand new restaurant for Mr Sabatini. Mr Sabatini asks Wendy to be the guest of honour at the grand opening. She is delighted but terrified at the prospect of giving a speech in public.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: An early morning start for the sunflower valley team, as they are moving logs in preparation for a safety fence around Mr Sabatini’s windmill. Wendy’s off to the sunflower oil factory to help Marjorie with a big delivery.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is tiling a roof and calls at JJ’s yard to pick up the materials. But there is only one pallet of the requisite red tiles in stock, so JJ promises to send the remainder of the order as soon as his delivery arrives.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: The Mayor asks Bob to build a Pets Corner in the local park. Farmer Pickles has picked up a selection of animals for it – including a chinchilla – and sends Travis and Spud off to deliver them to Bob. Unfortunately Bob runs out of chicken wire.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is installing an antique fireplace for Mrs Percival. He goes to JJ’s yard to collect it and finds JJ playing with his new model aeroplane. Bob then heads to Mrs Percival’s and starts to knock out the old fireplace.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is demolishing an old house, and sends the floorboards and roof tiles for recycling. As he finishes the job, he gets a call from Mr Bentley who wants him to renovate the museum’s annexe, where Mr Ellis plans to exhibit the work of local artists.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob has bought a new attachment for Benny to try. It looks a bit big and heavy, but Benny is desperate to try it out so Bob promises he can as long as he’s with him to help. Bob gets an excited phone call from Sandy Beech up at the cave house.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob is building a goat run for Farmer Pickles new goat – Giddy. It’s Travis’s job to collect Giddy from Meg MacDonald the goat breeder. Spud teases Travis about being slow and Travis resolves to collect the goat very quickly.

programme.title: Bob the Builder
programme.desc: Bob and the team are installing a bull-rush water recycling system to clean water from Farmer Pickles’ cottage. The clean water will be used to water Farmer Pickles new orchard. Bob explains that everything needs to be built in order – it’s a process.

programme.title: Green Builds and Recycling Centres
programme.desc: Bob’s back On Site! Join Bob as he shows you the amazing world of eco homes and recycling centres and learn how things you throw away can be used to make something new.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina: Episode: 19

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina
programme.desc: This episode includes: Who Killed Superman?, Satchel Bandit, Ghostbusters, Til Death Do Us Part, Money From Heaven and Hot Jock.

programme.title: Unsolved Mysteries with Dennis Farina
programme.desc: This episode includes: Diaz Dog Days (Pts. 1 & 2), Murder by Arson, Baby Broker and Lost Sailor.

programme.title: Halloween Special
programme.desc: Join Bex, Sean and the team as they investigate The Wellington Hotel for Halloween. Based in Halewood, this public inn has more haunting inside of its walls than the spirits behind the bar

programme.title: Ghost Dimension: The House of The Devil
programme.desc: The island of Gran Canaria houses some of the most haunted buildings in the world and tonight the team fly to the island and investigate claims of satanic worship and paranormal activity in a house that is believed to have been built by the Devil himself

programme.title: Ghost Dimension: House of Fear
programme.desc: Located in Gran Canaria, there is one house that locals refuse to enter and that is, The House of Fear. Ghostly shadows, loud banging and demonic voices have all been reported here and the Ghost Dimension team will investigate these claims tonight.

programme.title: Ghost Dimension
programme.desc: Ghost Dimension investigate ruins of The Orangery in Merseyside and claims that the ghost of a grey lady is haunting the location. They capture some of the most compelling paranormal evidence they have ever encountered.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Girl in The Ditch
programme.desc: When 12 year old Agnes Ali'iva'a drowned in a ditch in 1992, the police initially thought her death was accidental. It was four years before public and political pressure forced police to re-investigate her death.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Where's Grandad?
programme.desc: 65-year-old Alan Gordon Woodford of Bedford St. Mossburn left his home on foot Saturday morning April 20, 1985 and was never seen or heard of again.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: In Too Deep
programme.desc: In 1999, Kiwi engineer Aaron Hopa and his English colleague Robert Glazzard were murdered on board a survey ship in the Persian Gulf in.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: When The Trail Goes Cold
programme.desc: This episodes involves the disappearance of 62-year-old Kaye Stewart, a woman who went missing while on a short walk on June 13th, 2005.

programme.title: Sensing Murder: Fallen Angel
programme.desc: In this episode of Sensing Murder we learn about the murder of Angela Blackmoore, a 21 year old woman with a 2 year old child. The child was not harmed and the killer has not been found.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: A Fright At The Opera
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, the team anticipates the possibility of encountering a demonic entity at the Burlingame & Chaffee Opera House, in Carthage, Missouri.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: There Ghosts The Neighborhood
programme.desc: TAPSis called out to Joplin, Missouri to help 3 generations of afamilyliving on one haunted estate! Could both houses on the property harbor entities that couldbring harm to their children?

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: The Plot Thickens
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, owners of a haunted theatre in Woonsocket, Rhode Island buy the building next door and activity spikes! TAPS investigates both buildings to see if spirits are traveling between the two locations.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Fortress of Phantoms
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS heads tothe historic Fort Adams in Newport, Rhode Island. The teams investigate three deadly eventsthat surprisingly weren't from battle!

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Houghton Hears A Who?
programme.desc: TAPS investigates Houghton Mansionin North Adams, Massachusetts where the repercussions of a deadly car accident could be behind unexplained phenomena on the property.

programme.title: Ghost Hunters: Darker Learning
programme.desc: On this episode of Ghost Hunters, TAPS has been called to investigate Southern Vermont College in Bennington, Vermont. The legend of a mysterious love triangleand suicidescould be at the root of frightening paranormal activity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vegas
programme.desc: The PRS travels to Sin City to investigate a 14-year old medium who claims to speak to a dead girl.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Drowned
programme.desc: Kim and her family have been frightened out of their seaside Maryland home. Multiple family members have reported sightings of children with purple lips and strange noises emanating from the nearby creek.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Room 37
programme.desc: Darrell and Cherry, the owners of an historic Tennessee bed and breakfast built in the late 1800s, are reporting apparitions of a man and hearing whistling throughout the hotel corridors.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Haunted Connection
programme.desc: P.R.S. is called in to help a young mother tormented by an otherworldly connection to a teenage girl murdered in her neighborhood years ago. Could she have psychic ability or is there a more sinister force at work?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Family Ties
programme.desc: Jimmie is haunted by the apparition of a mysterious old woman who peers at him in the bathroom of his family's Petersburg, VA home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Requiem
programme.desc: A young woman named Kristy has been haunted in her own bedroom since she can remember. Now it's up to Ryan and the crew to help Kristy before she's overwhelmed by the entity.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Vendetta
programme.desc: P.R.S. investigates claims of supernatural revenge in the Pennsylvania Poconos. When a client's new boyfriend moves in, does the paranormal move in with him? Or did her ex put a hex on her house and the entire family?

programme.title: Paranormal State: Hostage to Fear
programme.desc: P.R.S. is summoned to help a family who has been haunted for years by spirits lurking in the house. Upon arrival the team finds a family that has become prisoners in their own home.

programme.title: Paranormal State: The Basement
programme.desc: A history of violence may connect the spiritual activity in Heidi's Ohio home to a long run of misery and cruelty.

programme.title: Paranormal State: Desperate Households
programme.desc: When Julie and her family moved into their dream home in suburban New Jersey they quickly realized they were not alone.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Todmoredon Church
programme.desc: For the first time Yvette takes her fearless Most Haunted team to an ordained church and graveyard. But it’s the cellar that proves the most fearful as the team get caught in a small room and they are not alone.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Weir Mill
programme.desc: Yvette take her team to an abandoned mill where spirits are said to roam and satanic ritual taken place. What awaits them is an evening of fear that causes one of the team to leave early.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Ripon Workhouse
programme.desc: In a place where the homeless were kept and work was hard, death was often the only escape but it seems that wasn’t the way for some as their souls are said to roam this place.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Ripon Prison
programme.desc: A small prison in Yorkshire gives a big impact when Yvette and her team attempt to stay the night. Although many inmates were released some are still locked up and they are not happy.

programme.title: Most Haunted: Standon Hall Part 1
programme.desc: Yvette Fielding and her team take on a three-part investigation that tests them to the very core. A place that surprised intrigued and terrified the hardened team, making Standon Hall the most fearful place they have investigated.

programme.title: Beyond Belief: Fact or Fiction: Episode 6
programme.desc: This episode features the following cases: Red Line, Two Sisters, Eclipse, The Ice Box, The Gathering.
